structure.text:
Subject: NCM-P-2025-03-20 Goldwater Bank Middle Income DPA
From: Nova Capital Markets <capitalmarkets@novahomeloans.com>
Date: 2025-03-20 15:33:14-07:00



ANNOUNCEMENT SUMMARY

 

Funds are now available for the Middle Income Down Payment assistance program through our partnership with Goldwater Bank!  

 

Eligible homebuyers who contribute a minimum of $10,000 toward their down payment may qualify for a grant of up to $50,000 that can be used for the homebuyer's down payment and closing costs. These funds can complement assistance from local, state, and federal homeownership programs and initiatives, providing even more opportunities for middle-income families to have access to an affordable home of their own. Keep in mind, the whole amount does not need to be used so the grant can be broken down and lowered.

 

 

Program/Borrower Eligibility

 

*	Be a first-time homebuyer <https://archives.hud.gov/offices/hsg/sfh/ref/sfhp3-02.cfm> , as defined by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD);
*	Income eligibility requires the homebuyer household to have earnings between 80.01% and 140% of the HUD Defined AMI <https://a-us.storyblok.com/f/1019683/x/0a5614bfc1/how-to-determine-100-hud-area-median-income-limits.pdf>  based on purchase property address and adjusted for household size (submit for income review prior to finding property)
*	Successfully complete a homebuyer counseling program.
*	Purchase an eligible property as their primary residence in Arizona, California, or Nevada. Such property may include 

	*	(1) single-family homes, such as a condominium, cooperative housing, and manufactured homes, and 
	*	(2) housing with two to four dwelling units with at least one owner-occupied unit.

*	Open escrow on a home purchase transaction, with closing scheduled within 30 days prior to the Bank member requesting program funds; and
*	Make a minimum contribution of $10,000, which may come from savings or gifted funds

 

As a reminder, this is a broker referral partnership, and the LO compensation and file workflow will follow the Brokered Loan Process.  An encompass file is needed for commission and reporting purposes, however, a Safe Harbor is not required. 

 

Important Note:

You must complete the attached checklist and send to Wilson Toft with any other borrower documentation. Please communicate with your borrower the partnership with Goldwater Bank and NOVA, and that NOVA has/receives permission from the borrower to share any emailed income/asset documentation that was not uploaded to the consumer connect Portal <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/goldwaterbank.com/nova-home-loans__;!!L9-43RkOeiO9WQ!QkBMyO8zHdI-EwuFBeZscB5KA8gZURkzTREJNaSSTSp3A7s3rcpYGTpgAUZUX7mxTF9PVaB2CUl4ZwdZiwDZtiWbdNgi4TNQXHS0pQ$> .

 

For pricing and program questions please contact our Goldwater Bank AE:

 

Wilson Toft | NMLS 2059497

Mortgage Loan Officer 

Goldwater Bank

Direct: 425-445-8119

wilson.toft@goldwaterbank.com <mailto:wilson.toft@goldwaterbank.com> 

 

 

Attachment:

*	MOSA Checklist

 

FOR MORE INFORMATION… 

*	For Underwriting inquiries, contact Scenario Desk <mailto:Scenariodesk@novahomeloans.com>  
*	For Product inquiries, contact Product <mailto:Productdept@novahomeloans.com> 
*	For Pricing inquiries, contact Lock Desk <mailto:lock.desk@novahomeloans.com> 

** For NOVA Home Loans Internal Distribution Only **

To find additional information, follow this path: NOVANet > Capital Markets <https://novahomeloans.sharepoint.com/sites/Home/SitePages/Capital-Markets.aspx>
